narrative.headline: 2022 Mazda CX-30: the engine tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 31 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 29%, followed by the steering and electrical system (19% each) and forward collision avoidance (16%). No fires and no deaths are reported. The steering complaints stand out for severity, with 2 crashes and 2 injuries from just 6 reports.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 9 reports (29%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Complaints point to oil consumption and low-oil warnings, so check the dipstick level.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 6 reports (19%) but the most serious by outcome: 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Worth a careful look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 6 reports (19%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. A recurring complaint area worth checking.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 5 reports (16%), with 1 crash and no injuries. Owners describe flawed behavior from the driver-assist features.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: The fewest reports at 3 (10%), but linked to 1 crash and 2 injuries. Watch for airbag or seatbelt warning lights on the dash.
